topic: What "one-stop" really means — and how to get there
hook: "One-stop" is the most valuable status in sync licensing.

slides.eyebrow: Education · What "one-stop" really means — and how to get there
slides.headline: "One-stop" is the most valuable status in sync licensing.
Most composers know the term.
slides.lede: Almost none fully understand how to get there — especially once a label is involved. · Here's the clear explanation. ↓ · ONE-STOP = you grant both licences from a single conversation.

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: LICENCE 1: The sync licence · Controls: use of your composition in visual media · Held by: the publisher — which is you, if you self-publish
list_items: Controls: use of your composition in visual media
Held by: the publisher — which is you, if you self-publish

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: LICENCE 2: The master licence · Controls: the specific recording · Held by: whoever owns the master — often a label, unless you recorded independently
list_items: Held by: whoever owns the master — often a label, unless you recorded independently

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede:
If you control both · one-stop.
If a label controls the master · the supervisor has to negotiate with two parties, on two timelines, with two chances for the deal to fall apart.
list_items:
one-stop.
If a label controls the master
the supervisor has to negotiate with two parties, on two timelines, with two chances for the deal to fall apart.

slides.headline: Supervisors strongly prefer one-stop.
slides.lede: When time is short (it always is), they go there first.

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: IF YOUR LABEL HOLDS YOUR MASTERS: · Check your deal: do you have sync approval rights? Even partial co-approval? · Negotiate co-approval on new work before signing · Build one-stop status into your next deal explicitly
list_items: IF YOUR LABEL HOLDS YOUR MASTERS:
Check your deal: do you have sync approval rights? Even partial co-approval?
Negotiate co-approval on new work before signing
Build one-stop status into your next deal explicitly

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: IF YOU'RE FULLY INDEPENDENT: · Distribute through a platform that doesn't claim master rights (most don't — but check) · Self-publish: register directly with your PRO · Document every track's rights status from day one
list_items: Distribute through a platform that doesn't claim master rights (most don't — but check)
Self-publish: register directly with your PRO
Document every track's rights status from day one

slides.headline: The path to one-stop is mostly paperwork.
slides.lede: It's the most commercial thing you can do for your catalogue right now.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
